summaries.128575: Comments confirmed reproduction on stable/master with CanvasKit on Chrome; Firefox is unaffected. Root cause identified as `Intl.Segmenter` being extremely slow on Chrome—iterating word segments over 3k lines takes ~1 second, scaling quadratically to 15 minutes for 100k lines. The issue is engine-level and was suggested to be tracked via a Chrome bug report. Status: stalled, no PR, previously labeled P1 with no active assignee.
summaries.178110: Comments confirm reproduction on both stable 3.35.7 and master 3.38. A commenter provides a workaround using `StrutStyle(forceStrutHeight: true)` sized to the largest span to prevent vertical cropping, plus `selectionHeightStyle: BoxHeightStyle.max`; no per-span cursor height support exists. Issue was triaged and cc'd to @LongCatIsLooong and @Renzo-Olivares. Status: triaged, no PR, workaround available.
summaries.129137: Comments confirmed reproduction on both stable and master on iOS (Chrome and Safari); HTML renderer exhibits the same behavior as CanvasKit. Root cause is that when the Flutter app is embedded in a div (not body), the scroll-into-view mechanism for text fields breaks on iOS when the virtual keyboard opens. Status: triaged for web team, no fix, stalled.
summaries.134358: Root cause identified in comments: the `contextmenu` event is not supported on iOS Safari per MDN docs, so `element.addEventListener('contextmenu', ...)` with `preventDefault()` cannot suppress the native context menu on mobile. Confirmed affected on both iOS (Safari) and Android (Chrome). No fix proposed; a commenter noted this may be related to native input work by @htoor3. Status: stalled, no PR.
summaries.84444: Comments narrowed the main repro: autofill suggestions disappear from the email field after tapping into the password field and back. Confirmed on iOS 14.6 on both stable and master. `AutofillGroup` was suggested as a workaround but reported as ineffective on iOS 16. A recent comment (iOS 17.6.1) could not reproduce the initial repro but a user still reports the field fills nothing when a suggestion is tapped. Status: stalled, intermittently reproducible, no PR.
summaries.149379: Comments narrowed the condition: the bug requires disabling macOS "Live Conversion" and using Google Japanese IME or the system IME with longer sentences. Confirmed macOS-only (not web). Related to #142493 and possibly resolved by #166291 via #160935. A commenter suggested `DeltaTextInputClient` as a workaround to observe per-keystroke changes; root cause is likely the engine incorrectly committing the composing region in `FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm`. Status: possibly fixed by a related PR, needs verification.
summaries.120351: Root cause identified: when the Samsung keyboard selects a suggestion like `[]`, it sends a batch edit containing (1) `commitText` replacing the composing text and (2) a `KEYCODE_DPAD_LEFT` key event to position the cursor inside the brackets. Key events are not currently treated as part of a batch edit, so the left-arrow arrives at the framework before the text replacement, causing state corruption. Analysis was documented in a shared Google doc. Issue was investigated by @Renzo-Olivares and handed off to @LongCatIsLooong for a more thorough fix requiring a non-trivial refactor. Status: assigned then unassigned, no PR landed, stalled.
summaries.110647: Comments confirmed the bug still reproduces on stable 3.3 and master 3.4; affects both iPhone and iPad (not just iPad). The issue is that hardware arrow keys don't navigate IME candidate lists for Chinese input on iOS/iPadOS—only the `+`/`-` keys work. No fix proposed; issue is 3+ years old with no activity. Status: stalled, no PR, open.
summaries.70947: Comments confirmed reproduction on stable and master via a provided video. The bug occurs when editing lower lines of a multiline TextField inside a NestedScrollView on Android—the current line scrolls out of the viewport. Issue #37721 flagged as possibly related. Status: triaged as P2, team-design, stalled.
summaries.91586: Comments clarified that the crash no longer occurs on master, but Shift+CapsLock still produces a `KeyUpEvent` assertion error (`_pressedKeys.containsKey(event.physicalKey)`) and causes Backspace/Delete to stop working in TextField afterward. Root cause is that the custom IME shortcut key sequence sends a synthesized KeyUp for CapsLock without a prior KeyDown. A team member noted proper handling exists for Alt+Shift but not for user-customized shortcuts. Status: partial fix (no crash), residual keyboard breakage remains, no PR.
summaries.137833: Comments confirmed reproduction on master without go_router when using CupertinoTabScaffold alone. Root cause identified: `CupertinoTabScaffold` calls `MediaQuery.of()` repeatedly in `build()`, even with `resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false`, triggering rebuilds every frame while the keyboard opens/closes, causing focus loss. Issue affects all text fields, not just CupertinoSearchTextField. Status: stalled, no PR, triaged as team-text-input.
summaries.126066: Comments confirmed the issue on macOS with Chrome/Edge using the system Chinese input method (HTML renderer); no issue on iPhone. Root cause appears shared with #120557 (composing region incorrectly committed mid-input). Triagers decided to keep this issue open due to clearer repro steps and to close #120557 as a duplicate. `canvaskit` renderer has an additional edge case with `InputFormatter`. Status: stalled, no fix, open.
summaries.91676: Comments confirmed reproduction on iOS simulator. Root cause is that `MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom` reports a value larger than the actual keyboard height on iOS, causing a gap between the keyboard top and dialog bottom. Possibly related to #80253. Issue cc'd to @jmagman for iOS team. Status: stalled, triaged iOS, no fix.
summaries.128689: Comments confirmed reproduction on Android, iOS, macOS, and web (both renderers). Root cause identified as TextField not accounting for characters with diacritics/accents—the text field's bounding box does not encompass the full glyph height, unlike the Text widget. Web offset is a separate rendering difference. Labeled P2 for consistency across platforms. Status: triaged, no PR, stalled.
summaries.100690: Comments confirmed the bug is specific to add-to-app (Flutter embedded in existing Android app) and affects Android OS <= 10; standalone Flutter apps and Android 11+ are unaffected. `MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom` stays at 0.0 when keyboard appears on Android 8/9. A commenter noted Android API 30 introduced behavioral changes. No fix proposed. Status: stalled, P2, team-android, no PR.
summaries.137659: Root cause identified by a commenter as likely related to #119583 (focus collision causing the framework to treat focus as Tab-triggered, selecting all text). Platform: web only; desktop is unaffected. Workaround provided: call `focusNode.requestFocus()` first, then set `controller.value` in a post-frame callback. Issue persists on stable 3.32 and linux (Flutter 3.32). Status: stalled, known workaround, no PR.
summaries.134926: Comments narrowed the issue to ATOK 2014 IME specifically; system IME and other IMEs work correctly on master channel. The misaligned composing underline is web-only and Windows desktop is unaffected. A triage commenter could not reproduce with system IME. Status: ATOK 2014-specific edge case, low priority, stalled.
summaries.96134: Root cause clarified: pasting multiline text loses line breaks on web when `keyboardType: TextInputType.text`; using `TextInputType.multiline` preserves them. A fix would require always using a `<textarea>` element even for single-line cases. Confirmed affected on web (Chrome and macOS desktop); macOS app unaffected. Status: stalled, workaround is to set `keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline`, no PR.
summaries.160418: Comments confirmed the focus-loss behavior occurs on Android with Gboard when the cursor moves near a `contenteditable=false` label element inside a `contenteditable` div in webview_flutter. iOS does not reproduce the issue. Triage notes the browser behavior is inconsistent (Firefox retains focus; Chrome/Edge do not). No fix proposed. Status: stalled, Android-only, triaged, no PR.
summaries.64552: Comments document no satisfactory workaround: `prefixIcon`/`suffixIcon` misaligns text, and suggested tricks with `FloatingLabelBehavior.never` or `suffixIconConstraints: BoxConstraints()` are acknowledged as hacks. The feature request is to allow `suffix`/`prefix` text to be always visible, analogous to Android's `setExpandedHintEnabled(false)`. Issue cc'd to @bleroux. Status: open feature request, P2, no PR.
summaries.122673: Comments confirmed the Insert key (overtype mode) does not work on web, Linux, or Windows; macOS has no Insert key. A team member noted this is not currently implemented and suggested using `Shortcuts` with `LogicalKeyboardKey.insert` as a workaround. A use case for a hex editor was offered. Status: unimplemented feature, gathering feedback, no PR.
summaries.162435: Comments confirmed the soft keyboard disappears on Android Chrome mobile web but not on iOS or native Android app. The issue requires both `Visibility` widget and `SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics()` to trigger. Status: stalled, reproduced on Android mobile web only, no fix, no PR.
summaries.161213: Root cause identified: `WidgetSpan` uses a Unicode object replacement character as a placeholder in `plainText`, not the user-supplied UTF-16 private-use codepoint, causing a mismatch in selection/caret offset calculations. A workaround is to use UTF-8 private use area (`E000~`) instead of supplementary plane (`F0000~`), which maps to a single UTF-16 code unit and is handled correctly. Investigation ongoing; a commenter is looking into fixing the selection logic. Status: workaround available (use BMP private use area), no PR, open.
summaries.131053: Comments confirmed reproduction on Android, iOS, web, and macOS. Root cause is a bug in `buildTextSpanWithSpellCheckSuggestions` that does not handle out-of-order or overlapping `SuggestionSpan` lists, corrupting the rendered text. A separate issue #132270 was filed for spell check not triggering until typing begins. @justinmc plans to fix the ordering/overlap bug and add a custom-service example. Status: stalled, no PR yet.
